segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: That must be hard, though.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: That's scary.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Well, thanks Coleman.
segments.zh: 嗯，谢谢科尔曼。我想我会去看看那个网站。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I'll go and have a look at that website, I think.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I never really planned to be a lifeboat volunteer when I came to live in Northsea.
segments.zh: 我当初来北海居住时，从没想过要当救生艇志愿者。我之前在伦敦做网站设计师，虽然工作有趣，但我不喜欢城市生活。我十几岁时就对船很着迷，想着如果住在海边，也许能在业余时间多发展这个爱好。后来发现救生艇协会在招志愿者，就决定申请了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I'd been working in London as a website designer, but although that was interesting, I didn't like city life.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I'd been really keen on boats as a teenager, and I thought if I went to live by the sea, I might be able to pursue that interest a bit more in my free time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Then I found that the Lifeboat Institution was looking for volunteers, so I decided to apply.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Lifeboat Institution building here in Northsea's hard to miss, it's one of the largest in the country.
segments.zh: 北海的救生艇协会大楼很难错过，它是全国最大的之一。15年前由一位在此居住了一生的慷慨市民捐款建造。由于救生艇协会是依赖此类捐款而非政府资助的慈善机构，那笔捐款对我们帮助巨大。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was built 15 years ago with funds provided by a generous member of the public who'd lived here all her life.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As the Lifeboat Institution is a charity that relies on that kind of donation, rather than funding provided by the government, that was a huge help to us.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When I applied, I had to have a health assessment.
segments.zh: 申请时，我做了健康评估。医生特别关注我的视力。我以前近视，得戴眼镜，但两年前做了激光手术，所以没问题。他们还给我做了色盲测试，以为我可能有问题，但结果没事。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The doctors were particularly interested in my vision.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I used to be short-sighted, so I'd had to wear glasses, but I'd had laser eye surgery two years earlier so that was OK.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They gave me tests for colour blindness and they thought I might have a problem there, but it turned out I was OK.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When the coastguard gets an alert, all the volunteers are contacted and rush to the lifeboat station.
segments.zh: 当海岸警卫队接到警报时，所有志愿者都会被联系并赶往救生艇站。我们的目标是在五分钟内到达，然后再用六到八分钟把船驶离码头出海。我们团队很自豪通常能做到——全国平均时间是八分半钟。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Our target's to get there in five minutes, then we try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Our team's proud that we usually achieve that – the average time across the country's eight and a half minutes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I've recently qualified as what's called a 'helmsman', which means I have the ultimate responsibility for the lifeboat.
segments.zh: 我最近获得了‘舵手’资格，这意味着我对救生艇负有最终责任。我必须检查所用设备是否正常——船员有特殊的救生衣，能在水中支撑多达四人。最终由我决定是否安全下水。但即使天气最恶劣，也很少不下水。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I have to check that the equipment we use is in working order – the crew have special life jackets that can support up to four people in the water.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And it's ultimately my decision whether it's safe to launch the boat.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But it's very rare not to launch it, even in the worst weather.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As well as going out on the lifeboat, my work involves other things too.
segments.zh: 除了出海救援，我的工作还包括其他事情。很多人低估了海上天气变化的速度，所以我经常跟当地的青年团体和帆船俱乐部讲，如果天气突然变坏，水手和游泳者可能会遇到哪些问题。我们还有很多志愿者组织活动为我们筹款，没有他们我们根本撑不下去。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A lot of people underestimate how quickly conditions can change at sea, so I speak to youth groups and sailing clubs in the area about the sorts of problems that sailors and swimmers can have if the weather suddenly gets bad.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We also have a lot of volunteers who organise activities to raise money for us, and we couldn't manage without them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The training we get is a continuous process, focusing on technical competence and safe handling techniques, and it's given me the confidence to deal with extreme situations without panicking.
segments.zh: 我们接受的培训是一个持续的过程，重点在于技术能力和安全操作技巧，这让我有信心应对极端情况而不慌张。我很庆幸在开始之前就学过急救课程，这对我们做的伤员护理工作帮助很大。我们花了很多时间学习如何处理绳索和打结——这是必备技能。一年后，我参加了一个为期一周的住宿培训，由专家指导。他们有一个造浪池，可以模拟极端天气条件——这样我们就能体验比如船在夜间风暴中倾覆时该怎么做。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I was glad I'd done a first aid course before I started, as that's a big help with the casualty care activities we do.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We've done a lot on how to deal with ropes and tie knots – that's an essential skill.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: After a year, I did a one-week residential course, led by specialists.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They had a wave-tank where they could create extreme weather conditions – so we could get experience at what to do if the boat turned over in a storm at night, for example.

items.prompt: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?

pairs.q: leave London and move to Northsea
pairs.p: I came to live in Northsea. I'd been working in London
pairs.note: 题干说离开伦敦搬到Northsea，原文说来到Northsea居住，之前在伦敦工作，意思一致。
题干问离开伦敦的原因，原文说‘不喜欢城市生活’，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头说‘I came to live in Northsea’和‘I'd been working in London’，对应题干‘leave London and move to Northsea’，接着‘I didn't like city life’直接给出原因。

items.prompt: The Lifeboat Institution in Northsea was built with money provided by

pairs.note: money和funds都表示资金，provided by是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问资金来源，定位句提到'funds provided by a generous member of the public'，与money provided by同义替换，且后文排除政府资助，因此选B。

items.prompt: In his health assessment, the doctor was concerned about the fact that David

pairs.note: concerned about 与 particularly interested in 都表示医生重点关注，是近义替换。

pairs.p: they thought I might have a problem
pairs.note: the fact that 引出具体问题，原文用 they thought I might have a problem 表达医生担忧的内容。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问医生担忧什么，定位句提到医生特别关注视力，并担心色盲问题，但最终没问题。答案应选与色盲相关的选项。

items.prompt: After arriving at the lifeboat station, they aim to launch the boat within

pairs.q: aim to launch the boat within
pairs.p: try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes
pairs.note: 题干'aim to launch the boat within'对应原文'try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes'，表示目标时间范围。

pairs.q: after arriving at the lifeboat station
pairs.note: 题干'after arriving at the lifeboat station'对应原文'get there in five minutes'，指到达站点的动作。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到志愿者到达站点需5分钟，然后尝试在6-8分钟内将船驶离码头出海，题干问的是'launch the boat within'的时间，即原文中的'get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes'，因此答案为B（6-8分钟）。

items.prompt: As a 'helmsman', David has the responsibility of deciding

pairs.note: 题干说‘决定的责任’，原文说‘我的决定’，意思相同但表达不同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问helmsman的责任是决定什么，原文中‘it's ultimately my decision’对应‘responsibility of deciding’，由此定位到答案。

items.prompt: As well as going out on the lifeboat, David

pairs.q: As well as going out on the lifeboat
pairs.p: As well as going out on the lifeboat
pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干开头与原文第一句完全一致，直接定位到该句。

items.prompt: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全相同的短语，直接定位。
题干关键词在原文中直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问David关于救生艇志愿者培训说了哪两件事，定位句开头直接提到'The training we get is a continuous process'，因此抓住'continuous process'和'training'即可定位。

items.prompt: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位
题干和原文完全一致，直接定位

paraphrase.explain: 题干问David关于救生艇志愿者培训说了哪两件事，原文中'continuous process'和'confidence'都是直接出现的，因此可以定位到这两点。

items.prompt: Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?
Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?
